base_text:
You allow yourself to feel superior, and you adopt a “I get it – you don’t” attitude. clown: the joker mode
I will joke you down; put you down by my joke. I can’t “talk with you”, so I will use my great sense of humor to put you in your place. The ploy masks hostility. listen to me: the me me me mode
If you try to interject a word while I am pontificating, I will ignore you as if you don’t exist. My words are much more important than any thing you can think of, so I will talk on-and-on-and-on at length without letting you sneak in a word edgewise.
final_text:

If you try to interject a word while I am pontificating, I will ignore you as if you don’t exist. My words are much more important than any thing you can think of, so I will talk on-and-on-and-on at length without letting you sneak in a word edgewise.

label: human
base_text:
In a few short years, the self-taught photographer has mastered her craft thanks to the help of the Paint Horses that dot her pasture in chestnut and white. Photo Frenzy
Now 33, Holley first began experimenting with photography back in 2005—her interest was spurred by images that seemed to jump off magazine pages, and horses were her chosen muses. “Posters in magazines were a big factor because the horses always looked so perfect,” she said. Holley admired noted equestrian photographer Bob Langrish’s work and says his calendar photography was also influential in getting her started.
final_text:

Holley admired noted equestrian photographer Bob Langrish’s work and says his calendar photography was also influential in getting her started.

label: human
base_text: The Argentine soccer star said on Twitter that his father was released without harm. "I thank everyone for their support always. I'm able to tell you my father is OK, safe and sound. That's what's most important," he said. "I also thank ... the Buenos Aires and federal and anti-kidnapping police [forces] for their support for my family." The daily La Nacion, quoting sources close to the police operation, reported the kidnappers had told Tevez's father: "We admire your son, but we have to do this to work". The amount paid for Segundo Tevez's release was not revealed but media reports said it was 400,000 Argentine pesos ($52,076).
final_text: The Argentine soccer star said on Twitter that his father was released without harm. `` I thank everyone for their support always. I 'm able to tell you my father is OK, safe and sound. That 's what 's most important, '' he said. `` I also thank... the Buenos Aires and federal and anti-kidnapping police [ forces ] for their support for my family. '' The La Nacion, quoting sources close to the police operation, reported the kidnappers had told Tevez 's father: `` We admire your son, but we have to do this to work ''. The amount paid for Segundo Tevez 's release was not revealed but media reports said it was 400,000 Argentine pesos ( $ 52,076 ).
